title: Rate Limiter
description: Implement a token bucket rate limiter. The first line of input is 'rate=N' (tokens per second, also the bucket capacity). Each subsequent line is a request timestamp in milliseconds. Process requests in order: refill floor(elapsed_ms * rate / 1000) tokens since the last event, then allow the request if tokens >= 1 (deduct 1 token), else throttle. Output ALLOWED or THROTTLED per request.
constraints: First line: rate=N (integer tokens per second, bucket capacity = rate)
Bucket starts full at capacity = rate
For each request at timestamp T: refill = floor((T - last_T) * rate / 1000), capped at capacity
If tokens >= 1 after refill: allow request, deduct 1 token, output ALLOWED
If tokens < 1: output THROTTLED (do not deduct)
Output format: '<timestamp> ALLOWED' or '<timestamp> THROTTLED', one per line

examples.explanation: rate=1. Bucket starts at 1. ts=0: bucket=1, allow (bucket=0). ts=10: refill=floor(10*1/1000)=0, bucket=0, throttle. ts=20: same. ts=1000: refill=floor(980*1/1000)=0 then full from ts=0 -> refill=floor(1000/1000)=1, bucket=1, allow (bucket=0). ts=1010: refill=0, throttle.

test_cases.description: rate=2, all requests allowed with enough spacing
rate=1, rapid bursts are throttled, then allowed after refill
rate=3, burst of 3 allowed, 4th throttled, then refill allows 3 more
test_cases.expected_output:
0 ALLOWED
100 ALLOWED
200 ALLOWED
300 THROTTLED
1000 ALLOWED
1100 ALLOWED
1200 ALLOWED
1300 THROTTLED
